elections.note: Certified final 589,611 ballots cast (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Stats by County: polls 306,324 + VBM 283,287; 69.24% of 851,581 registered). Pre-adoption (precinct-based; e-pollbooks not until 2020). Wayback/press-release/DocumentCenter/social-handle routes exhausted (no election-night results.aspx capture exists; see prior dead-end history). RECOVERY ATTEMPT (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank cross-check via Press-Enterprise, SB Sun's own 2012 coverage in-window has no countywide figure): 'Many races await tallies' (Nov 9 2012, docref news/14275EF93A805950): 'About 60,000 mail-in and 45,000 provisional ballots in San Bernardino County were uncounted as of Thursday morning, said Elections Office spokeswoman Felisa Cardona. Ballots cast at polling places and mailed prior to Election Day were all counted by 5:15 a.m. Wednesday, six hours faster than the 2008 presidential election, Cardona said.' 589,611 minus 105,000 (60,000+45,000) = 484,611 = 82.19% -- well ABOVE the general ~55-75% precinct-era band and above SF's own 2012 figure (71%), with no precinct-era SB anchor available for comparison. CEILING, not a confirmed plateau: the 60,000/45,000 remaining figure is explicitly anchored to 'as of Thursday morning' (a Wednesday-or-later post-night state per RUNBOOK 5.2), not a confirmed held-since-election-night figure with a no-update bracket (unlike this county's own 2018/2024 rows and Riverside's 2012/2014/2016/2018 rows); SB, unlike Riverside in the same article, offered no 'not counting today' pause confirmation, so counting plausibly moved between the 5:15 a.m. Wednesday polls+early-mail completion and the Thursday-morning quote. No earlier (Wednesday-dated) SB-specific figure was found in either paper. Retained as a documented CEILING (upper bound on the true, lower, election-night count), comparable:false, confidence secondary.

Certified final 293,283 ballots cast (CA SoS; polls 117,179 + VBM 176,104; 34.44% of 851,684 registered). Pre-adoption, precinct-based. Election-night PLATEAU not sourceable: the live iframe results page (www.sbcounty.gov/rov/elections/Results/20141104/content/results.aspx) has NO election-night Wayback capture. The earliest archived rendering is a POST-CANVASS capture from Fri Nov 7, 2014 17:48 PST (3 days after Election Day) showing Precinct Turnout 107,774 + VBM 172,331 = 280,105 -- already 95.5% of the 293,283 certified final, i.e. days of canvass updates had already been added; it is explicitly a multi-day canvass figure, not the election-night plateau, so it cannot be used. No earlier (Election-Night) capture exists. Null per definition. snapshot 20141108014824 (post-canvass, not usable). [2026-06 re-check per updated methodology: no pre-2019 downloadable election-night report PDF/XLSX exists for this election, and San Bernardino ROV issues no morning-after 'semi-final results' ballot-count press release (full news archive 2011-2026 = public notices/certifications only); re-CDX of content/results.aspx on the www.sbcounty.gov host confirms the earliest capture is Fri Nov 7 17:48 PST (the 280,105 post-canvass figure), with no Election-Night capture. Remains null.] DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether San Bernardino's elections CMS (sbcounty.gov/rov, sbcountyelections.com, results.rov.sbcounty.gov) or its parent domain runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.sbcounty.gov/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @SBCountyROV via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; alternate handle guesses (SBCountyElex, SBCountyElections) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for this county's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above. RECOVERY ATTEMPT (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank cross-check via Press-Enterprise; SB Sun's own in-window coverage has no countywide figure, only a district-specific CD-31 remaining count): 'a new low in area's Voter turnout?' (docref news/151717B8F7250120): 'Less than 30 percent of registered voters cast ballots in the Inland area, according to preliminary numbers released Wednesday by county registrars ... In San Bernardino County, turnout was 27.1 percent. San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters Michael Scarpello estimated that the final number likely will be closer to 34 percent' (matches actual certified 34.44% almost exactly). 27.1% x 851,684 registered = 230,806. 230,806/293,283 = 78.70% -- anomalously high, similar to this county's 2012 row. CEILING, not confirmed: the figure is explicitly 'released Wednesday' (post-night per RUNBOOK 5.2), and this row's own already-documented posting schedule (resultsTimeline.html) shows SB updates precinct counts 'every 2hrs' through Wednesday, meaning the Wednesday figure plausibly already includes several post-election-night updates. Retained as a documented CEILING (upper bound), comparable:false, confidence secondary.

Certified final 672,871 ballots cast (CA SoS; polls 287,315 + VBM 385,556; 75.77% of 888,019 registered). Pre-adoption, precinct-based. Election-night PLATEAU not sourceable: San Bernardino's live iframe results page for 2016 (www.sbcounty.gov/rov/elections/Results/20161108/) was archived only as the frameset default.html (Fri Nov 11 19:27 PST) plus a POST-CANVASS capture of the inner content/results.aspx on Fri Nov 18, 2016 (10 days after Election Day) -- the iframe data pages were never crawled at an election-night timestamp (the 8:05pm/10pm/etc. live postings overwrote in place and were not separately archived). No clean end-of-night county ballot total found in news. Null per definition. snapshot 20161118181952 (post-canvass, not usable). [2026-06 re-check per updated methodology: no pre-2019 downloadable election-night report PDF/XLSX exists, and San Bernardino ROV issues no morning-after 'semi-final results' ballot-count press release (full news archive 2011-2026 = public notices/certifications only); re-CDX of content/results.aspx confirms the earliest capture is Fri Nov 18 (post-canvass), no Election-Night capture. Remains null.] DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether San Bernardino's elections CMS (sbcounty.gov/rov, sbcountyelections.com, results.rov.sbcounty.gov) or its parent domain runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.sbcounty.gov/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @SBCountyROV via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; alternate handle guesses (SBCountyElex, SBCountyElections) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for this county's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above.

Certified final 546,041 ballots cast (CA SoS; polls 209,168 + VBM 336,873; 58.26% of 937,316 registered). Pre-adoption, precinct-based (LAST midterm before e-pollbooks in 2020). Wayback/press-release/DocumentCenter/social-handle routes exhausted (only a post-canvass Nov 12 capture of 365,083 exists; see prior dead-end history). RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): 'Elections - Many Inland ballots still not processed or counted' (docref news/16F9051D0AED5530): 'Final unofficial vote counts aren't expected to be released until ... 4 p.m. Friday for San Bernardino County. In San Bernardino County, elections workers counted about 322,000 ballots overnight Tuesday. On Wednesday, about 150,816 ballots remained to be counted, along with 8,929 damaged ballots and 43,818 provisional and other ballots that require processing. There are 942,474 registered voters in San Bernardino County, so the unofficial turnout estimate is about 55 percent.' DIRECT statement (not a subtraction derivation): 'counted about 322,000 ballots overnight Tuesday' is squarely the election-night plateau. Cross-check: Wednesday's remaining total (150,816+8,929+43,818=203,563) implies a slightly higher Wednesday-state count (546,041-203,563=342,478) than the overnight-Tuesday 322,000, consistent with modest same-day Wednesday processing before the article's data cutoff (SB's own final unofficial count was not due until 4 p.m. Friday) -- not a contradiction. Turnout-estimate cross-check: (322,000+203,563)/942,474=55.76%, matching the article's own 'about 55 percent' almost exactly, and the eventual certified 546,041/942,474=57.94% is close too. PLATEAU = 322,000 (directly stated). 322,000/546,041 = 58.97%, within the ~55-75% precinct-era band. Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-quoted registrar figure, round number).

Certified final 458,946 ballots cast (CA SoS; polls 66,469 + VBM 392,477; 40.30% of 1,138,818 registered). Post-epollbook (KNOWiNK PollPad from 2020), pre-ASV. Election-night PLATEAU not sourceable despite knowing its exact posting time: San Bernardino's archived 2022 Results Posting Schedule (sbcountyelections.com/Elections/2022/1108/ResultSchedule/, capture 20221118191058) shows the 'Posting of Final Unofficial Election Night Results' was at 9:40 a.m. Wed Nov 9, 2022, with the canvass resuming at the 4 p.m. Wed posting. But Wayback never captured the live results page (sbcountyelections.com/elections/2022/1108/results/, which embeds results.rov.sbcounty.gov/Results/20221108/) in the 9:40am-4pm Wed window. The only night capture is the 4:00 a.m. Wed INTERMEDIATE posting (capture 20221109134003 = Wed 05:40 PST), which the page itself labels '4:00 a.m. Posting of Unofficial Election Night Results' and shows Voters Cast 177,481 of 1,138,702 at only 1,365 of 2,765 precincts (49.37%) -- a mid-count undercount (38.7% of certified), NOT the plateau. Surrounding captures are pre-poll-close (Tue 18:34) and post-canvass (Nov 27). No clean 9:40am plateau total recoverable. Null per definition (the 4 a.m. figure is the classic first-tranche/mid-count trap; not substituted). snapshot 20221109134003 (4 a.m. intermediate, not the plateau). [2026-06 re-check, report-file + press-release routes per updated methodology: the page's downloadable ElectionSummaryReportRPT.pdf was NEVER archived (CDX = 0 captures), and the corresponding .xlsx was captured only pre-poll-close (Tue Nov 8 13:03 PST, near-zero/template) and post-canvass (Thu Nov 10 22:06 PST) -- never the Wed Nov 9 9:40 a.m. plateau window; a prefix CDX of all results.rov.sbcounty.gov/Results/20221108 captures dated Wed Nov 9 returns ONLY the single 05:40 PST '4 a.m. posting' page (Voters Cast 177,481 of 1,138,702, 15.59% of registered -- confirmed from the raw 1.9 MB archived HTML). San Bernardino ROV also issues no morning-after 'semi-final results' ballot-count press release (full news archive 2011-2026 = public notices/certifications only). Remains null.] DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): checked whether San Bernardino's elections CMS (sbcounty.gov/rov, sbcountyelections.com, results.rov.sbcounty.gov) or its parent domain runs CivicPlus DocumentCenter -- it does not (CDX prefix probe of www.sbcounty.gov/DocumentCenter: 0 captures), so no ID-walk was possible. Checked the brief's named registrar Twitter handle @SBCountyROV via Wayback CDX (exact URL and matchType=prefix, unrestricted date range): zero captures under any form; alternate handle guesses (SBCountyElex, SBCountyElections) also returned zero. No Facebook page candidate for this county's registrar was found either. No new route found; row remains as documented above.

Election-night PLATEAU = 434,108 ballots ('Voters Cast: 434,108 of 1,198,556', 36.22% of registered) at Precincts Reported 2,872 of 2,872 (100.00%), from San Bernardino's OFFICIAL Clarity-style results page whose header reads literally 'Final Unofficial Election Night Results' with 'Next Update: November 6, 2024 4:00 p.m.' -- recovered from Wayback capture 20241106224114 (Wed Nov 6, 2024 2:41 PM PST). The county's archived Results Posting Schedule (Elections/2024/1105/ResultSchedule/, capture 20241106231234) pins the '10 a.m. - Posting of Final Unofficial Election Night Results' (Wed Nov 6) as the last election-night posting, with the canvass resuming at the 'Next update - Wednesday, November 6, 2024, 4 p.m.' posting -- so this 2:41 PM capture sits squarely on the plateau, between the 10 a.m. final-EN posting and the 4 p.m. canvass resume. Composition confirms pre-canvass: Mail 299,226 + Designated Mail 1,028 + Polling Place 133,854 + Provisional 0 = 434,108 (provisionals not yet processed). The corroborating downloadable ElectionSummaryReportRPT.pdf on results.rov.sbcounty.gov, crawled earlier at Nov 6 09:28 PST, shows a still-rising intermediate (417,655 at 58.67% precincts) -- so 434,108 is the higher, settled 100%-precinct plateau, not an undercount. 434,108 / 771,834 certified = 56.24% (matches SF's ~57% calibration for 2024). Post-epollbook (2020), pre-ASV (2025). snapshot 20241106224114 used. Numerator render-verified from the archived JS results page (puppeteer; see render_verified.json).

notes: Definition: election-night % = ballots in San Bernardino County's LAST election-night report -- the 'Final Unofficial Election Night Results' posting (the cumulative 'Voters Cast' total at 100% of precincts that plateaus and holds the day after Election Day, before the next-scheduled afternoon posting resumes the multi-day canvass) -- divided by CA SoS certified final ballots cast (Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County). Covers the November generals 2012, 2014, 2016, 2018, 2022, 2024 that bracket San Bernardino's 2020 adoption of electronic pollbooks (KNOWiNK PollPad, first used March 2020) and its 2025 adoption of Automatic Signature Verification (ASV, first used Nov 2025 special); the 2020 general is deliberately EXCLUDED as a COVID early-voting outlier. ADOPTION: e-pollbook 2020 -> 2012/2014/2016/2018 are PRE, 2022/2024 POST; ASV 2025 -> all six are PRE. DENOMINATORS are PRIMARY and solid for all six (CA SoS SOV participation tables; each cross-checks against its registered-voter turnout %). NUMERATOR is recoverable for only ONE election: 2024 presidential = 434,108 (100% precincts, the page-labeled 'Final Unofficial Election Night Results' posted 10 a.m. Wed Nov 6, captured 2:41 PM Nov 6 before the 4 p.m. canvass resume), giving 56.24% -- PRIMARY. The other five have no archived election-night plateau: 2012/2014/2016/2018 used a JS-iframe results page (www.sbcounty.gov/rov/elections/Results/<date>/) whose dynamic iframe data was never crawled at an election-night timestamp -- only post-canvass captures survive (2014 Fri Nov 7 = 280,105/95.5% of final; 2016 Fri Nov 18; 2018 Mon Nov 12 = 365,083/66.9% of final). 2022 (post-epollbook) is the most frustrating: the archived posting schedule pins the 'Final Unofficial Election Night Results' to 9:40 a.m. Wed Nov 9, but Wayback only captured the 4 a.m. intermediate posting (177,481 at 49.37% precincts -- a mid-count undercount) and post-canvass, never the 9:40am-4pm plateau window. So a clean pre/post-e-pollbook election-night comparison CANNOT be made for San Bernardino from archived first-report data -- only the single post-e-pollbook 2024 value (56.24%) is sourceable, and no pre-e-pollbook value is. (Method note: WebFetch is blocked for web.archive.org but curl/CDX --noproxy and the headless-Chrome render_wayback puppeteer helper reach it; San Bernardino self-hosts a Clarity-style results viewer on results.rov.sbcounty.gov, surfaced via sbcountyelections.com pre-2024 and elections.sbcounty.gov in 2024, with downloadable ElectionSummaryReportRPT.pdf/.xlsx from 2019 onward.)
